emails.body:
GOVERNMENT EXECUTIVE DAILY BRIEFING
October 1, 2026

TOP STORIES

📅 APPROPRIATIONS CALENDAR: Senate returns Monday with continuing resolution deadline looming
The Senate convenes Monday at 3 p.m. with just 30 days until the fiscal year ends. Leadership aims to pass a clean CR through mid-December, but defense hawks are pushing for a full-year bill. House Appropriations Chair signals willingness to negotiate if Senate moves first.

🏛️ AGENCY OVERSIGHT: GAO releases high-risk list update
GAO added three programs to its biennial high-risk list: DOD's financial management modernization, VA's electronic health record deployment, and DHS's cybersecurity talent pipeline. DOD's acquisition workforce gaps remain on the list for the seventh consecutive cycle.

⚖️ LEGAL: Supreme Court declines to hear major questions doctrine challenge to EPA rule
The Court's denial leaves the D.C. Circuit's ruling intact, preserving EPA's authority under the Clean Air Act. Justice Gorsuch wrote a brief dissent joined by Justice Alito, arguing the case presented a vehicle to clarify West Virginia v. EPA.

📊 WORKFORCE: OPM issues new telework guidance for 2027
Agencies must submit updated telework plans by Dec. 15. New metrics include "mission impact assessments" for positions designated remote-eligible. Union leaders call the guidance a step backward; management groups welcome the flexibility.

🔗 QUICK LINKS
• Senate calendar: www.congress.gov/schedule
• GAO high-risk report: www.gao.gov/highrisk
• OPM telework memo: www.opm.gov/policy

GOVERNMENT EXECUTIVE DAILY BRIEFING
October 4, 2026

TOP STORIES

🛒 ACQUISITION REFORM: Bipartisan Senate bill targets middle-tier acquisition authorities
Sens. Warner (D-VA) and Ernst (R-IA) introduced the Acquisition Agility Act, which would codify MTA pathways, require quarterly reporting to Congress, and create a pilot for "rapid fielding" authorities modeled on DOD's Section 804. Industry groups cautiously optimistic; watchdog groups want stronger cost controls.

🤖 AI GUIDANCE: OMB releases M-26-15 implementation memo for federal AI governance
Memo requires agencies to designate Chief AI Officers by Jan. 15, 2027, complete risk assessments for high-impact AI use cases by March 31, and publish annual AI inventories. DOD and IC get extended timelines for national security systems. NIST AI RMF 2.0 referenced as baseline framework.

💰 BUDGET: CBO scores defense supplemental at $98B over ten years
The estimate assumes 3.2% inflation and includes $12B for munitions replenishment, $8B for shipbuilding acceleration, and $5B for Indo-Pacific posture initiatives. Senate Armed Services markup scheduled for next Thursday.

🏗️ INFRASTRUCTURE: GSA announces Federal Capital Revolving Fund first awards
$1.2B across 14 projects, including FBI headquarters consolidation, VA hospital modernization in Louisville, and NOAA research facility in Charleston. Fund designed to reduce long-term lease costs through federal ownership.

🔗 QUICK LINKS
• Acquisition Agility Act text: www.congress.gov/bill/s-4892
• OMB M-26-15: www.whitehouse.gov/omb/memos
• CBO defense supplemental score: www.cbo.gov/publication/59821
• FCRF awards list: www.gsa.gov/fcrf
